description_en: The action of gathering together as a group for a common purpose or the fitting together of parts.
example: The final assembly of the engine takes place in this factory.
note: Refers to both people and manufacturing.

description_en: Large in quantity or amount.
example: Buying in bulk can often save companies a lot of money.

meaning: hãng vận chuyển; người chuyên chở
description_en: A person or company that undertakes the professional conveyance of goods or people.
example: Which shipping carrier do you prefer for international delivery?
note: Includes airlines, shipping lines, and trucking companies.

description_en: The action of delivering letters, packages, or goods.
example: The estimated delivery time for your order is three business days.
note: Final stage of the logistics process.

description_en: Send (goods or services) to another country for sale.
example: Japan exports many high-tech electronics around the world.

description_en: Goods transported by truck, train, ship, or aircraft.
example: Air freight is much faster but more expensive than sea freight.

meaning: cổng kết nối; trạm trung chuyển
description_en: An entrance or a means of access.
example: The port of Singapore is a major gateway for trade in Southeast Asia.

meaning: kinh doanh vận tải đường bộ
description_en: The commercial transport of goods by road.
example: The company specializes in long-distance heavy haulage.

description_en: Bring (goods or services) into a country from abroad for sale.
example: The country needs to import more fuel to meet energy demands.

description_en: A point where two or more things are joined.
example: Turn right at the next major junction.
note: Meeting point of roads or railways.

description_en: The SI unit of mass.
example: The maximum weight for a checked bag is 23 kilograms.
note: Standard unit of weight in logistics.

description_en: The detailed coordination of a complex operation involving many people, facilities, or supplies.
example: Efficient logistics are critical for the success of any e-commerce business.

description_en: A document listing the cargo, passengers, and crew of a ship, aircraft, or vehicle.
example: The customs officer checked the ship's manifest.

description_en: A group or system of interconnected people or things.
example: The airline is expanding its international flight network.

description_en: The action of making the best or most effective use of a situation or resource.
example: Route optimization can help reduce fuel consumption and costs.

description_en: A flat wooden structure used as a base for handling or moving goods.
example: The forklift moved the heavy pallet into the warehouse.

description_en: A stone or metal platform lying alongside or projecting into water for loading and unloading ships.
example: The cargo ship was docked at the main quay.

description_en: Income, especially of a company or organization.
example: The logistics giant reported a 10% increase in annual revenue.

description_en: A quantity of goods sent by sea, air, or land.
example: Your shipment has been dispatched and should arrive in two days.
note: A specific batch of transported goods.

description_en: The carry of people, goods, or materials from one place to another.
example: The goods were damaged while in transit.

meaning: tia cực tím (dùng trong khử trùng container)
description_en: Light beyond the violet end of the visible spectrum.
example: UV sterilization is used to clean surfaces in food logistics.

description_en: A ship or large boat.
example: The port handles over 500 cargo vessels every month.

description_en: A large building where raw materials or manufactured goods may be stored before their export or distribution for sale.
example: Goods are stored in the warehouse until they are ready for delivery.

meaning: tia X (để kiểm tra an ninh hàng hóa)
description_en: A form of electromagnetic radiation used for imaging.
example: All international shipments must pass through an x-ray scanner.

description_en: A piece of ground used for a particular occupation or business.
example: The trucks are parked in the transport company's storage yard.

description_en: An area or stretch of land having a particular characteristic, purpose, or use.
example: The free trade zone allows for duty-free imports.

description_en: The flying or operating of aircraft.
example: Technological advances have made aviation safer than ever.

meaning: hàng tồn đọng; công việc tồn đọng
description_en: An accumulation of uncompleted work or matters needing to be dealt with.
example: The port strike caused a massive backlog of shipping containers.
note: Accumulation of delayed tasks or goods.

meaning: sự thông quan; khoảng cách an toàn
description_en: Official permission for something to proceed.
example: The shipment is still waiting for customs clearance.

description_en: A place where large quantities of ground resources are stored or where vehicles are housed and maintained.
example: The buses return to the city depot at the end of every day.

description_en: The state or quality of being efficient.
example: Improved supply chain efficiency can significantly reduce costs.
note: Doing things in the best way.

description_en: A group of ships, vehicles, or aircraft sailing or traveling together.
example: The company operates a fleet of over 200 delivery trucks.

description_en: The total weight of goods and their packaging.
example: The manifest must list the gross weight of every pallet.

meaning: trung tâm; trạm trung chuyển chính
description_en: The effective center of an activity, region, or network.
example: Dubai is a major international aviation hub.

description_en: A planned route or journey.
example: The driver's itinerary includes three stops before noon.
note: Detailed path and schedule for a journey.

meaning: đúng lúc (phương thức quản lý hàng tồn kho)
description_en: A manufacturing system in which items are produced or delivered only as they are needed.
example: Using a just-in-time system reduces the need for large warehouse space.

meaning: hải lý trên giờ (đơn vị đo tốc độ trên biển)
description_en: A unit of speed equal to one nautical mile per hour.
example: The vessel was traveling at a speed of 20 knots.
note: Standard speed unit for ships and planes.

meaning: tải trọng; hàng hóa được chở
description_en: A weight or source of pressure.
example: The truck was carrying a full load of construction materials.

meaning: số dặm đã đi; mức tiêu thụ nhiên liệu
description_en: A distance in miles traveled or covered.
example: The car has a low mileage for its age.
note: Tracked distance a vehicle has traveled.

meaning: công chứng viên (trong chứng từ vận tải)
description_en: A person authorized to perform legal formalities.
example: International shipping documents often need to be certified by a notary.

description_en: Traveling away from a particular place.
example: We are processing all outbound shipments for the afternoon delivery.

description_en: A maritime commercial facility where ships may dock to load and unload passengers and inventory.
example: The port was busy with cargo ships from all over the world.

description_en: A state, period, or place of isolation in which people or animals that have arrived from elsewhere are placed.
example: Livestock must go through a period of quarantine before entering the country.
note: Used to prevent disease spread in transport.

description_en: A track made of steel rails along which passenger and freight trains run.
example: The railway is a cost-effective way to transport bulk goods.

description_en: A person whose occupation is to build and repair ships.
example: The experienced shipwright inspected the hull for damages.

description_en: Monitoring the movement and location of something.
example: Customers can use their tracking number to see where their package is.

description_en: Charge (someone) too little for a good or service.
example: The logistics company realized they had an undercharge in the last invoice.

description_en: A person or thing that validates something.
example: The ticket validator at the station was out of order.

description_en: A river, canal, or other route for travel by water.
example: The Panama Canal is a vital international waterway.

meaning: nhường đường; lợi nhuận vận tải
description_en: Give way to other traffic.
example: Drivers must yield to pedestrians at the crosswalk.
